justification: All three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts). CouponRepository is explicitly a pure interface. CouponService injects the repository via constructor. All 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED). Tests use mock/stub repository with 8 test cases covering all 5 ACs. files_changed lists all three files with purposes. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific test names.

observations.text: All three files created with correct separation of concerns per MJ's spec
CouponRepository is a pure interface, not a concrete class
All 5 ACs covered with explicit test case names (AC1-AC5 labeled)
Error codes match Bird's spec exactly: COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_NOT_FOUND, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED
AC5 (single-use) not overlooked — tested both positive and negative cases
Boundary testing included for AC1 (fractional) and AC4 (exact minimum)

Validation order differs slightly from rubric expected (AC3→AC2→AC5→AC4→AC1) vs spec (AC3→AC2→AC5→AC4→AC1) — actually matches, but output states NOT_FOUND→EXPIRED→ORDER_MINIMUM→ALREADY_USED, placing AC4 before AC5 instead of AC5 before AC4
Uses inline assertions rather than a standard test framework — functional but less conventional
